source: Asynchronous Training of Word Embeddings for Large Text Corpora

concepts.finding: Extends word2vec to distributed asynchronous training with parameter servers. Analyzes gradient staleness, learning rate decay, and negative sampling distribution in async setting. Achieves near-linear speedup with up to 64 workers while maintaining embedding quality. Key insight: stale gradients are less problematic than expected for embedding training.
concepts.implication: Large-scale keyword embedding training (millions of keywords, billions of co-occurrences) can be distributed across many workers with asynchronous SGD. Keyword co-occurrence matrices at scale require distributed training infrastructure.
concepts.open_question: At what scale of keywords does distributed async training become necessary? What negative sampling distribution works best for keyword co-occurrence data?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Asynchronous training of keyword embeddings achieves 50x speedup with 64 workers while maintaining >95% of the cluster quality of single-worker training.
